'''Dean Whare''' pronounced (FAR-dEH) (born 22 January 1990) is a New Zealand professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a halfback for the [[Glebe Dirty Reds]] in the NSWRL [[Ron Massey Cup]], and [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] and the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] at international level.

Whare previously played for the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] and the [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], and played as a {{rlp|wg}}er and {{rlp|fb}} earlier in his career.

==Early years==
Whare was born in [[Rotorua, New Zealand]]. He is of [[Māori people|Māori]] descent.{{cn|date=July 2025}}

He moved to Sydney, Australia as a 13-year-old and played his junior rugby league with Penshurst RSL and [[Arncliffe scots]] before being signed the [[St George-Illawarra Dragons]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.nzrl.co.nz/kiwis-player-profiles/dean-whare.aspx |title = NZRL |archive-url=https://archive.today/20150218044242/http://www.nzrl.co.nz/kiwis-player-profiles/dean-whare.aspx |archive-date=18 February 2015 |url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsportspulse.com/club_info.cgi?client=1-2154-21117-0-0&sID=221000&&news_task=DETAIL&articleID=20876563|title=International Honours For Scots Boy Dean Whare – Arncliffe Scots|publisher=Fox Sports Pulse|access-date=23 December 2015}}</ref>

==Playing career==
He played in the 2008 and 2009 [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|Toyota Cups]] before signing with the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] for 2010.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/news/news/heat-is-on--brown-and-his-players-know-its-time-for-dragons-tostart-breathing-fire/2008/02/24/1203788145615.html|title=Heat is on – Brown and his players know it's time for Dragons to start breathing|website=Smh.com.au|date=25 February 2008|access-date=23 December 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://manly-daily.whereilive.com.au/sport/story/be-whare-the-rookie/|title=Be-Whare the rookie|access-date=7 July 2011|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120315203732/http://manly-daily.whereilive.com.au/sport/story/be-whare-the-rookie/|archive-date=15 March 2012 }}</ref>

===2010===
In round 12 of the [[2010 NRL season]], Whare made his [[NRL]] debut against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] at [[1300SMILES Stadium]], scoring three tries in Manly's 24–20 win, the first time a player has scored three tries in his top-grade debut for the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/rookie-sea-eagle-whare-scores-hat-trick-after-last-minute-call-up/story-e6frexni-1225872982332|title=Rookie Sea Eagle Whare Scores Hat-Trick After Last Minute Call|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=23 December 2015}}</ref> Whare played in 4 matches and scored 3 tries in his debut year in the NRL.

At the end of the season, Whare was selected for the [[Junior Kiwis]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/NewsViews/LatestNews/NewsArticle/tabid/10874/newsId/60572/Default.aspx|title=Nine NYC-winning Warriors named in Jnr Kiwis|publisher=NRL.com|access-date=23 December 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160130173736/http://www.nrl.com/NewsViews/LatestNews/NewsArticle/tabid/10874/newsId/60572/Default.aspx|archive-date=30 January 2016|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2011===
[[File:Dean Whare.jpg|thumb|right|Whare playing for the Sea Eagles in 2011]]
Whare played in one match for the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] in the [[2011 NRL season]], playing in round 26 against the [[Brisbane Broncos]] at [[Suncorp Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|author=Steve Mascord|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/thaiday-broncos-face-anxious-wait-over-tackle-20110904-1js59.html|title=Thaiday, Broncos face anxious wait over tackle|website=Smh.com.au|date=4 September 2011|access-date=23 December 2015}}</ref>

===2012===
In 2012, Whare divided his playing time between the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] [[NRL]] side, and the [[New South Wales Cup]]. This was due to Manly's established backline which included representative players [[Brett Stewart (rugby league)|Brett Stewart]], [[Steve Matai]], team captain [[Jamie Lyon]], [[Jorge Taufua]] and [[David Williams (rugby league)|David Williams]]. Becoming something of a "Mr Fix-it" for the Sea Eagles when called into the top grade, Whare played in the centres, on the wing, and at fullback during the season. On 30 May 2012, Whare announced that he had signed for the [[Penrith Panthers]] from 2013, citing the opportunity to establish himself as one of the NRL's top centres. Despite expressing a desire to stay at Manly beyond 2012, Whare felt his opportunities to play centre was limited at Manly due to the presence of club co-captain [[Jamie Lyon]] and [[New Zealand national rugby league team|Kiwi]] international centre [[Steve Matai]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://manly-daily.whereilive.com.au/sport/story/whare-signs-with-penrith/|title=Whare Signs With Penrith|website=Manly-daily.whereilive.com.au|access-date=23 December 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/nrl/capped-out-penrith-panthers-sign-manly-sea-eagles-utility-dean-whare-after-appealing-to-nrl-for-concessions/story-e6frfgbo-1226373989098|title=Panthers Sign Whare After Appealing to NRL for Concessions|website=Heraldsun.com.au|access-date=23 December 2015}}</ref> Whare finished the [[2012 NRL season]] with playing in 21 matches and scoring six tries for the Manly club.

Whare was selected to make international debut for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] in the October test at [[1300SMILES Stadium]]. Whare played at centre in the Kiwis 12–20 loss.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.nzrl.co.nz/news-archive/grand-finalists-dominate-kiwis-squad-for-townsville.aspx |title = NZRL |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130206171916/http://nzrl.co.nz/news-archive/grand-finalists-dominate-kiwis-squad-for-townsville.aspx  |archive-date=6 February 2013|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/kangaroos/australia-grind-it-out-in-second-half-to-win-18-10-over-new-zealand-in-townsville-test-match/story-fn2mcuhw-1226495026875|title=Australia Grind It Out to Win 18–10|website=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=23 December 2015}}</ref>

===2013===
In round 1 of the [[2013 NRL season]], against the [[Canberra Raiders]], Whare made his club debut for the [[Penrith Panthers]] at fullback in a 32–10 win at [[Penrith Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/new-look-penrith-panthers-pounce-on-tired-canberra-raiders-in-scorcher/story-fn2mcuj6-1226594184465|title=New Look Penrith Panthers Pounce on Tired Canberra Raiders in Scorcher|website=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=23 December 2015}}</ref> Whare started the season playing at fullback until he cemented a centre position from round 7 onwards. In round 7, against the [[Parramatta Eels]], Whare scored his first try club try for Penrith in the 44–12 win at Penrith Stadium.<ref>{{cite web|author=Michael Chammas|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/suspected-broken-leg-to-mannah-compounds-eels-woes-20130429-2ip5a.html|title=Suspected broken leg to Mannah compounds Eels' woes|website=Smh.com.au|date=29 April 2013 |access-date=23 December 2015}}</ref> For the [[2013 ANZAC Test]], Whare was selected to play for New Zealand at centre in the Kiwis 12–32 loss against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/australia-beats-new-zealand-32-12-at-canbera-stadium/story-e6frexnr-1226624627487|title=Australia Beat New Zealand 32–12|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=23 December 2015}}</ref> Whare finished the [[2013 NRL season]] with him playing in all of Penrith's 24 matches and scoring five tries. Whare was selected in the New Zealand [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|2013 World Cup]] squad, playing in 5 matches. He scored 2 tries against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] in the Kiwis 56–10 win.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/new-zealand-v-png-player-ratings/tabid/10874/newsid/75328/default.aspx|title=New Zealand v PNG player ratings|website=NRL.com|date=9 November 2013|access-date=23 December 2015}}</ref> Whare played at centre in the Kiwis 2–34 loss to Australia in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup final|World Cup final]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/world-cup-final-player-ratings/tabid/10874/newsid/75602/default.aspx|title=World Cup final player ratings|website=NRL.com|date=1 December 2013|access-date=23 December 2015}}</ref> On 13 December 2013, Whare agreed to a three-year contract extension with the Penrith Panthers, until the end of the 2017 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://m.nrl.com/whare-a-panther-through-2017/tabid/10874/newsid/75748/default.aspx|title=Whare a Panther through 2017 - NRL.com|website=M.nrl.com|date=13 December 2013|access-date=23 December 2015|archive-date=24 December 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151224104503/http://m.nrl.com/whare-a-panther-through-2017/tabid/10874/newsid/75748/default.aspx|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>[http://www.sportal.com.au/league/nrl-premiership/dean-whare-commits-to-penrith-panthers-for-three-more-years/b9rnad9dbwm31qc98bmmkdcd5] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131216143112/http://www.sportal.com.au/league/nrl-premiership/dean-whare-commits-to-penrith-panthers-for-three-more-years/b9rnad9dbwm31qc98bmmkdcd5|date=16 December 2013}}</ref>

===2014===
Whare was selected for New Zealand in the [[2014 ANZAC Test]] against Australia at centre in the Kiwis 18–30 loss at the [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|SFS]].<ref>{{cite web|author=Chris Barrett|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/australia-get-the-victory-but-new-zealand-take-the-plaudits-after-giving-world-champions-a-scare-20140502-zr3lc.html|title=Australia get the victory but New Zealand take the plaudits after giving world champions a scare|website=Smh.com.au|date=2 May 2014|access-date=23 December 2015}}</ref> Whare played in 26 matches and scored 12 tries in the Panthers [[2014 NRL season]].

On 7 October 2014, Whare was selected in the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]] final 24-man squad for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations series]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/four-rookies-in-kiwis-four-nations-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/82419/default.aspx|title=Four rookies in Kiwis Four Nations squad|website=NRL.com|access-date=23 December 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150923193201/http://www.nrl.com/four-rookies-in-kiwis-four-nations-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/82419/default.aspx|archive-date=23 September 2015|url-status=dead}}</ref> Whare played at centre in the Kiwis 22–18 Four Nations final win over Australia at [[Westpac Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/four-nations-2014-new-zealand-champions-after-defeating-australia-2218-in-wellington/story-fni3fbgz-1227124298670|title=Four Nations 2014 : New Zealand Champions After Defeating Australia 22–18|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=23 December 2015}}</ref>

===2015===
Whare finished the [[2015 NRL season]] with him playing in 13 matches and six tries for Penrith. 

On 8 October, Whare was selected in the 23-man [[2015 New Zealand rugby league tour of Great Britain|New Zealand squad to tour England]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/kiwis-name-squad-for-england-tour/tabid/10874/newsid/90473/default.aspx|title=Kiwis name squad for England tour|website=NRL.com|date=8 October 2015|access-date=20 December 2015}}</ref> 
[[File:Dean Whare Kiwis.jpg|thumb|right|Whare playing for the Kiwis in 2015]]
Whare played in all 3 matches against [[England national rugby league team|England]] at centre in the Kiwis' 2–1 [[Baskerville Shield]] series loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/england-v-new-zealand-3rd-test-hosts-take-series-with-nervy-20-14-win-over-kiwis-in-wigan/story-fni3fbgz-1227609556526|title=England v New Zealand : Third Test Hosts Take Series With Nervy 20–14 Win Over Kiwis in Wigan|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=23 December 2015}}</ref>

===2016===
Whare played in the 18–16 loss at [[Penrith Stadium]] in round 2 against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]]. This was his only match of the season after battling a calf tear injury before a season-ending knee injury happened during training.<ref>{{cite web|author=Nathan Ryan |url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/panthers-and-new-zealand-lose-dean-whare-to-possible-acl-injury/news-story/9ec092f78f1157c4e26319ae670de796 |title=NRL: Panthers and New Zealand lose Dean Whare to possible ACL injury |work=[[Fox Sports]] |date=28 April 2016 |access-date=30 March 2017}}</ref>

===2017===
In round 1, against the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]], Whare made his return for Penrith playing on the wing in the 10–42 loss at [[Jubilee Oval]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/whare-benefits-from-injury-layoff/tabid/10874/newsid/104166/default.aspx |title=Whare benefits from injury layoff |website=NRL.com |date=3 March 2017 |access-date=30 March 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/dragons/st-george-illawarra-dragons-defeat-penrith-panthers-4210-in-round-one-boilover/news-story/52510a54f2034112b7a22cbb8734fc7f |title=Dragons Death Panther 42–10 in Round One Boilover |website=Dailytelegraph.com.au |access-date=30 March 2017}}</ref>

===2018===
Whare made 24 appearances for Penrith in the [[2018 NRL season]] as the club finished 5th on the table and qualified for the finals.  Whare played in both finals games as Penrith reached the elimination final against Cronulla-Sutherland but lost 21–20 at the [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]] which ended their season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/09/19/penrith-panthers-2018-season-review/|title=Penrith Panthers 2018 season review|website=www.nrl.com|date=24 October 2018 }}</ref>

===2019===
Whare made a total of 18 appearances for Penrith in the [[2019 NRL season]] as the club finished a disappointing 10th place and missed out for the finals for the first time since 2015.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://westernweekender.com.au/2019/09/blame-game-will-not-solve-panthers-problems/|title=Blame game will not solve Panthers' problems|website=Western Weekender|date=10 September 2019 }}</ref>

===2020===
Whare only made a total of 8 appearances for Penrith in the 2020 NRL Season, failing to score a try as he lost his spot to [[Brent Naden]] for the rest of the season after Round 10 as the Panthers won 17 consecutive matches to reach the grand final, which they lost 26-20 to the [[Melbourne Storm]].{{cn|date=March 2024}}

===2021===
On 8 February, it was reported that he had signed for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=8 Feb 2021|access-date=8 Feb 2021|title=Dean Whare: Catalans Dragons sign centre from Penrith|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55979155}}</ref>

In round 1 of the 2021 Super League season, he made his debut for Catalans Dragons against [[Hull KR]] in which Catalans won 29-28.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12258696/steve-mcnamara-hails-debutant-mathieu-laguerre-after-catalans-defeat-against-hull-kr/|title=Steve McNamara hails debutant Mathieu Laguerre after Catalans defeat against Hull KR|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

In round 5, Whare scored two tries for Catalans in their victory over [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/tom-davies-hat-trick-and-dean-whare-double-helps-catalans-cruise-past-wakefield/|title=Catalans v Wakefield Trinity match report|website=www.totalrl.com|date=30 April 2021 }}</ref>
On 9 October,  Whare played for Catalans in their [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] defeat against St. Helens.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
===2022===
On 24 October 2022 it was reported that he had signed for [[Baroudeurs de Pia XIII]] in the [[Elite One Championship]]. After the conclusion of the season, Whare joined RFL Championship side the London Broncos.<ref>{{cite web|website=Examiner.co.uk|date=24 Oct 2022|accessdate=25 Oct 2022|title=Catalans Dragons centre and former Wigan Warriors prop set for French league move|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/dean-whare-mitch-clark-pia-25342070.amp}}</ref>

===2023===
On 15 October, Whare played in the London Broncos upset [[Million Pound Game]] victory over [[Toulouse Olympique]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67105947|title=Championship Grand Final: Toulouse 14-18 London Broncos - Capital club back in Super League|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

===2024===
On 16 January it was reported that he had returned to [[Pia Donkeys]] in the [[Elite One Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=16 Jan 2024|title=Former Kiwi international, NRL stalwart set for France return after successful RFL Championship stint – reports|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-kiwi-international-nrl-stalwart-set-for-france-return-after-successful-rfl-championship-stint-reports}}</ref>
On 14 September, Whare played in Glebe's Ron Massey Cup grand final victory over [[Wentworthville Magpies|Wentworthville]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2024/09/14/david-nofoaluma-double-helps-glebe-dirty-reds-to-ron-massey-cup-premiership/|title=Nofoaluma double helps Glebe to Ron Massey Cup title|website=www.nswrl.com.au}}</ref>
